name: Black Bean Thin Crust Dough
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup black bean puree (if using canned beans, rinse and drain first)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in a bowl. If using a bread machine, mix according to the manufacturer's instructions.
2. Let the dough rise for 50-60 minutes.
3. Preheat the oven to 500 degrees Fahrenheit (260 degrees Celsius).
4. Roll out the dough on a cornmeal-covered surface.
5. Place the rolled-out dough in a pizza pan.
6. For a thin, crispy crust, add toppings and bake immediately.
7. For a thicker, chewier crust, let the dough rise for an additional 15-30 minutes before adding toppings.
8. Place the pizza on the bottom rack of the oven and bake for 5-10 minutes.
9. Fill with pizza sauce, remaining black beans, tomatoes, zucchini, onions, etc.
10. Optional: Create pizza dough chips by rolling out leftover dough into square shapes and baking them in the oven. The next day, use these chips to dip your favorite Bean Salsa (recipe not provided). Note that the chips may puff up like puff balls; consider pricking each chip with a fork before baking for better results.
